verdicts.note: Direction is right but strength 2 is out of line with this file's own scale. The dossier establishes this is a REPLACEMENT of an expiring levy with >70% of proceeds continuing existing service, and the '17% expansion' in the basis is measured against the mayor's proposal, not against the expiring 2019 levy. Skykomish's replacement levy was mapped at 1 on continuation logic, and the 2-strength mappings elsewhere are a near-doubling lid lift (FD47) and a brand-new district-tax structure (Black Diamond). A renewal-plus-growth levy belongs at 1.

review_notes: Attacked mappings: Black Diamond taxes 2 holds (permanent levy of $1.00/$1,000 plus a non-voted benefit charge versus a sub-$0.50 contract cost is a genuine step-change, and the con case is centrally about the new tax); Black Diamond local-control 2 holds and is the rubric's named exemplar (city-negotiated contract traded for a regional board). FD43 local-control 1 was the closest call — annexation actually ADDS voter representation, which cuts toward local say — but the structural change is still consolidation of an independent district into a regional authority, and the honest 1-strength basis already concedes the ambiguity. FD43 correctly carries no taxes mapping (pamphlet does not itemize post-annexation rates; computing one would import facts from the parallel Black Diamond coverage). Snoqualmie Pass's empty lean_mappings is correct: a fee-for-tax structural swap with an offsetting $0.50/$1,000 levy-authority giveback has no confident direction on any axis. Display text: all cost_lines match dossier figures ($1,084/2,790 sq ft/$754k; $277.50/$750k; $0.23-$193-$9/month; $0.46/$1,000; $0.45 vs $0.61); every pro/con summary names its actual pamphlet signatories; the FD47 and Snoqualmie 'pro' texts correctly attribute claims to the district's official explanatory statement rather than inventing an advocacy committee; all four no-statement-filed notices match the pamphlet record. The Skykomish pro_summary correctly declines to repeat the committee's inaccurate 'maintains the same current rate' claim (the dossier shows $0.45 vs $0.61); Covington's con utility-tax claim is presented as the opponent's attributed assertion, which the pro rebuttal disputes — acceptable as attributed.
